summary: Three canonical sources disagree on MS baseline direction at game-time. ms_trajectory_v30 §2 says Force-1 baseline continuity is 'always positive' (toward 100); clock_registry_v30 §MS says MS '0-100, start 72, direction down (decay)'; sim/peninsular/ms_track.py L44 sets MS_BASELINE_DECAY_PER_YEAR = -1 (decay, applied year-end). clock_registry + sim agree (down); ms_trajectory prose says up. Load-bearing on the single terminal world-state. Source: loop_doublecheck_DC1-12.md DC-2 (2026-05-28); unresolved as of this consolidation.
remediation: Jordan decision: either game-time net-decay is intended (post-Solmund, Wardens at nadir) -> annotate ms_trajectory §2 'always positive' as historical-only and not game-time; or the sim baseline is a sign error -> fix. NOT Claude's to resolve (load-bearing world variable + metaphysical layer).
note: ID is a placeholder. Jordan assigns ED-NNN on ratification (next free appears >= ED-883; not self-assigned due to the ED-865/866/867 + ~94-ID collision backlog).

summary: Should game-time have PASSIVE Warden Mending, or is action-gated Mending the intent? MS does not self-heal at game-time (the historical climb was Solmund + Einhir-survivor Wardens, both gone at start; P-07: the substrate does not self-repair). With no active Mending agent, sustained war drives MS to its single terminal at ~60x baseline -- canon-faithful, not a bug (R6 death-spiral was substantially correct). Gates the MS-loop NERS-R verdict (the loop is bounded IFF the -10/s cap holds AND a Mending agent is active). Source: DC-1 (2026-05-28).
remediation: Jordan decision (design + metaphysical layer): passive Warden Mending vs action-gated Mending. Also confirm the -10/s MS net-loss cap (CONTESTED, DC-2) -- the MS NERS-R verdict is conditional on it.
note: Decision item, not a defect. Placeholder ID; Jordan adjudicates.
